description:
MDI maintains its own subscription concept (separate from Stripe's billing subscription) — it governs clinical refill cadence. We create one in MDI immediately after Stripe subscription activation and cross-link them.

**Scope**
- `src/lib/mdi/subscriptions.ts` — wrappers for `POST /partner/subscriptions`, `GET /partner/subscriptions/:id`, `PATCH /partner/subscriptions/:id`, `POST /partner/subscriptions/:id/cancel`.
- Mapping is **1:1** with Stripe: `subscriptions.mdi_subscription_id` ↔ `subscriptions.stripe_subscription_id`. Each is written into the other's metadata for cross-lookup.
- Worker handler for MDI subscription webhooks: status mirror updates.
- Cancellation: a Stripe `customer.subscription.deleted` triggers MDI cancel via T-027's flow; an MDI `subscription_cancelled` triggers Stripe cancel-at-period-end.
- Pause: `invoice.payment_failed` (dunning) pauses MDI subscription rather than cancelling — gives the patient a payment-update window.

**Acceptance**
- Activation at `case_completed` (T-058) creates an MDI subscription with the right cadence; cross-IDs land in both metadata fields.
- Cancelling on either side propagates within one webhook cycle.
title: MDI subscription lifecycle (refill cadence)
